reasoning: This word is glossed as picking up or measuring out an amount purse-hand.
This corresponds to placing an object so that it is on its face.
This term relates to piercing and thus covers a threaded pierce.
Kaleer means ‘to scoop out strips of a vegetable.’
Koorq is given as ‘to fall off (as a fruit does).’
Kleer is the instrument used for scooping out strips.
Krneep is given as ‘to take someone across.’
Krnoorq is described as tongs or pincers.
Paraang corresponds to ‘to pick something off.’
The modified sense is ‘to cross over.’
Basal form that, when modified, can mean ‘to cross over.’
